sealLevels.label: No Sovereignty
sealLevels.description: Service, technology, or operations under exclusive non-EU control and non-EU jurisdiction.

sealLevels.label: Jurisdictional Sovereignty
sealLevels.description: EU law formally applies with limited enforceability and significant non-EU control.

sealLevels.label: Data Sovereignty
sealLevels.description: EU law is enforceable with material non-EU dependencies remaining.

sealLevels.label: Digital Resilience
sealLevels.description: EU law is enforceable and EU actors exercise meaningful influence with only marginal non-EU control.

sealLevels.label: Full Digital Sovereignty
sealLevels.description: Technology and operations are under complete EU control with no critical non-EU dependencies.

objectives.name: Strategic Sovereignty
objectives.description: Anchoring of services within EU legal, financial, and industrial ecosystems.
contributingFactors: Location of decisive authority within EU jurisdiction
Assurances against change of control
EU financing, investment, jobs, and value creation
Alignment with EU strategic initiatives
Operational continuity during support disruption

questions.prompt: How strongly are decisive governance and control bodies located under EU jurisdiction?
How robust are legal and organizational safeguards against non-EU change of control?
How strong is sustained EU industrial participation (investment, jobs, initiatives)?

objectives.name: Legal and Jurisdictional Sovereignty
objectives.description: Extent to which service governance is anchored in EU jurisdiction and shielded from external legal claims.
contributingFactors: National legal system governing contracts and operations
Exposure to non-EU extraterritorial laws
Channels through which non-EU authorities can compel access
Applicability of international transfer restrictions
Jurisdiction and location of IP development

questions.prompt: To what extent are contracts and operations governed exclusively by EU law?
How limited is exposure to non-EU laws with cross-border reach?
How effectively are legal, contractual, and technical access channels from non-EU authorities prevented?

objectives.name: Data and AI Sovereignty
objectives.description: Control and protection of data and AI processing under EU governance.
contributingFactors: Customer control over cryptographic access
Auditability of access and AI usage
Irreversible deletion with evidence
EU-only storage and processing confinement
EU control of model and data pipeline lifecycle

questions.prompt: How strong is customer-exclusive control over cryptographic keys and access rights?
How complete is EU confinement for data storage, processing, and AI model operation?
How auditable are access events, AI model usage, and verified data deletion outcomes?

objectives.name: Operational Sovereignty
objectives.description: Ability of EU actors to operate and evolve technology without foreign dependence.
contributingFactors: Ease of migration and avoidance of lock-in
EU operator capability without non-EU support
Availability of EU talent pool
EU-jurisdiction operational support
Availability of full documentation and know-how

questions.prompt: How practical is workload migration or integration with alternative EU-controlled solutions?
How independently can EU teams operate, maintain, and support the service?
How complete is access to technical documentation, source materials, and operational know-how?

objectives.name: Supply Chain Sovereignty
objectives.description: Geographic origin, transparency, and resilience of critical technology supply components.
contributingFactors: Geographic source of hardware and manufacturing
Jurisdiction and provenance of firmware and embedded code
Origin and jurisdiction of software development and distribution
Dependence on non-EU vendors and facilities
Visibility into suppliers and sub-suppliers

questions.prompt: How strong is EU provenance and legal control across critical hardware and firmware components?
How strongly is software architecture, packaging, distribution, and updates governed from the EU?
How transparent and auditable is the full supplier and sub-supplier chain?
questions.factor: Supply chain visibility and audit rights

objectives.name: Technology Sovereignty
objectives.description: Openness, interoperability, auditability, and independence in core technology stacks.
contributingFactors: Use of open and documented standards
Access through non-proprietary APIs and protocols
Open licenses with audit and modification rights
Architectural transparency and dependency visibility
EU independence in HPC ecosystem components

questions.prompt: How well does the solution interoperate through non-proprietary standards, APIs, and protocols?
How open and auditable is software access, including rights to inspect and adapt where applicable?
How visible are architecture and dependencies, including critical compute ecosystem reliance?

objectives.name: Security and Compliance Sovereignty
objectives.description: Extent to which security operations and compliance execution remain under EU control.
contributingFactors: Attainment of recognized EU and international certifications
Adherence to GDPR, NIS2, DORA and related frameworks
EU-jurisdiction SOC and incident response control
Transparent breach and vulnerability reporting
Independent EU audit and maintenance autonomy

questions.prompt: How complete is compliance with relevant EU security and regulatory frameworks?
How strongly are monitoring, SOC, and incident response functions controlled under EU jurisdiction?
How independently can EU entities audit, patch, and sustain secure operations over time?

objectives.name: Environmental Sustainability
objectives.description: Long-term sustainability and resilience from energy, materials, and environmental impact perspectives.
contributingFactors: Energy efficiency and measurable targets
Circular economy practices
Transparent reporting of emissions and water usage
Sourcing of renewable or low-carbon energy

questions.prompt: How strong are measurable improvements in infrastructure energy efficiency and PUE targets?
How mature are lifecycle and circular economy practices for equipment reuse and end-of-life handling?
How transparent are environmental disclosures and renewable or low-carbon energy sourcing?
